_meta.purpose: Transport class determination rules. Field AI's transport_class output MUST match the class returned by the first matching rule, OR be a more conservative escalation. Set membership against enum enforced by safety filter.
_meta.content_provenance: AI-generated rule set aligned with NIMS ICS resource typing concepts, WHO MCM transport prioritization, and AMPA (Air Medical Physician Association) HEMS criteria. NOT a validated EMS dispatch matrix. Real deployment must conform to local EMS protocols.
ref_categories_used: AMPA HEMS Indications (Air Medical Physician Association)

special_classes_explanation.refuse_transport_treat_in_place: Patient cannot be safely moved due to scene hazard. Treat where patient is, await scene clear.
special_classes_explanation.team_withdraw_recall: Scene is dangerous to rescuers. ALL rescuers withdraw. Mark patient location for re-entry. This OVERRIDES all patient-level transport decisions.
special_classes_explanation.no_transport_marker_only: Patient is deceased (BLACK tag). Mark location, do not occupy transport. Only valid if triage_tag == BLACK.

_meta.matching_rules: Rules are evaluated TOP TO BOTTOM. First match wins. Place most specific / most conservative rules FIRST.

match_fields.any_risk: rule matches if patient.hidden_risks_fired contains ANY of the listed Q codes
match_fields.all_risk: rule matches only if ALL listed Q codes are in hidden_risks_fired
match_fields.any_injury: rule matches if visible_injuries contains ANY listed
match_fields.environment: rule matches if environment is in the listed set

rules.reasoning: Scene poses immediate threat to rescuers. Withdraw, mark patient location, await HazMat clearance. Patient-level transport cannot proceed.
Deceased patient. Mark location and document. Do not occupy transport resources during active MCI.
Critical patient with chemical exposure. Must complete decontamination before transport to avoid contaminating receiving facility.
Time-critical critical injury with extended ground transport time. HEMS preferred if available within 20 min. Otherwise default to ALS ground.
Critical obstetric emergency requires OB-capable facility. HEMS preferred if available; otherwise ALS to nearest OB capable.
Critical instability with hidden risk requires ALS-level airway management, IV access, drug administration, and continuous monitoring.
rules.ref_category: NIMS ICS Resource Typing + WHO MCM

rules.reasoning: Critical pediatric patient requires ALS with pediatric-equipped unit. Consider PHEMS if available.
All critical patients default to ALS for monitoring + intervention capability during transport.
Hidden risk should normally auto-upgrade triage to RED. If risk present but tag remains YELLOW, transport at ALS level pending re-evaluation.
Chemical exposure requires decon prior to transport regardless of patient acuity.
Stable urgent patient. BLS adequate. May mix-load up to 2 YELLOW per ambulance per local protocol.
Even GREEN patients require decontamination before bus transport. Use BLS with decon-aware crew.
Ambulatory or minor. Group transport (bus, van, multi-patient vehicle) acceptable to preserve ambulance capacity for higher-acuity.
Cannot safely move patient out of active collapse zone. Treat where patient is, await USAR clearance for extrication path.
Fallback rule when no specific match. BLS default. Manual review recommended.
